articles.title: Mercury drops after rain-hailstorm; alert issued for 25 districts today: Heatwave warning amid pre-monsoon ... - Bhaskar English
articles.full_text:
Mercury drops after rain-hailstorm; alert issued for 25 districts today:Heatwave warning amid pre-monsoon activity; know about the weather changes
The pre-monsoon season has started well in Rajasthan. From the evening of June 4 to the morning of June 5, up to 3 inches of rain fell in several districts, including Banswara, Bundi, and Bhilwara. Hailstorms also occurred in Churu, Hanumangarh, and Bikaner.
Due to storms and rain, the maximum temperature has dropped by up to 5 degrees. The temperature in several cities, including Karauli, Dausa, Dungarpur, Nagaur, and Jaipur, has come down below 35°C.
The Meteorological Department has issued a storm and rain alert for 25 districts on Saturday. However, from June 8, there is a warning of intense heat and heatwave once again in the western parts of the state.
PHOTOS of Rajasthan's weather..
These cities remained the hottest
Updates related to heat and rain
- Jaisalmer remained the hottest: In the last 24 hours, the highest temperature of 42.1°C was recorded in Jaisalmer. Apart from this, temperatures of 41.5°C in Sriganganagar, 40.8°C in Barmer, 39.5°C in Jodhpur, 39.2°C in Bikaner, 38°C in Jalore, Churu, and Pali, 37.5°C in Hanumangarh, 37.2°C in Pilani, and 37°C in Udaipur were recorded.
- Mercury dropped due to rain: A decline in temperature was recorded due to rainfall between June 4 and 5 in areas of Jaipur, Ajmer, Udaipur, Bharatpur, Kota, and Bikaner divisions. The highest rainfall of 75 MM (about 3 inches) was recorded in Shahpura of Bhilwara. Apart from this, 45 MM in Ajmer, 34-34 MM in Nainwa of Bundi, Kachola of Bhilwara, and Bayana of Bharatpur, and 31-31 MM rainfall was recorded in Bhainsrodgarh of Chittorgarh and Weir of Bharatpur.
- Strong storm in the afternoon, hail fell with rain: On Friday, the weather suddenly changed in the afternoon in the areas of the Bikaner division. Dense clouds covered Bikaner, Hanumangarh, and Churu areas, and along with strong dusty winds, stormy rain occurred at many places.
Weather in major cities of Rajasthan
Jaipur:
Kota: The effect of intermittent rain on Thursday night was visible on Friday. After the rain, a significant drop in the city's temperature has been recorded. On Friday, the maximum temperature was 35.9°C, and the minimum temperature was 22.4°C. In the last 24 hours, 10 millimeters (mm) of rain have been recorded here. People have got relief from the heat.
Sikar: On Friday, the sun remained intense throughout the day. However, due to the effect of dust storms and rain in recent days, a drop of about 5°C in temperature has been recorded. The maximum temperature was recorded at 36.5°C, while a day earlier on Thursday, the maximum temperature here was 41.2°C.
Jodhpur:
Alwar: Dense clouds covered the district until around 1 PM. The maximum temperature of the district was recorded at only 36°C. By evening, clouds covered the sky once again, and the weather remained pleasant. There is an alert for rain with strong winds in the district until Sunday.
Udaipur:
Ajmer:
